rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF DAMISELA VADO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Accounting: a. The accounting petition does not contain a report. b. Conservator obtained the proceeds of the life insurance policy, which appears to have been $31.970.64, and which he distributed 1/2 to himself and 1/2 to a Vinicio Escobar. c. There are no allegations as to what happened to the 80 shares of Wells Fargo stock or the $682.43 in cash, or if there is anything left in the estate now. d. Cash has not been verified. e. Cash withdrawals and creditor card payments totaling $2,100 are shown without any explanation. f. Conservator reimbursed himself $440 without any explanation. g. $1,008 was "paid in error - repaid", but there is no receipt of $1,008 shown in the accounting. 2) Proposed order has not been submitted. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCIS KUTTNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/7/25. Examiner's Notes sent on 6/3/25 with the following issues: 1) There is over $390K cash in one bank (Wells Fargo). Proof of full FDIC coverage must be filed prior to the estate closing. 2) Petitioner alleges that Decedent's income taxes and fiduciary income taxes have not been filed yet. As Decedent died in 2023, please allege why they have not been filed. 3) Petitioner requests $100K in reserve without sufficient justification. 4) Proposed distribution of stocks must be specific (e.g. to John Doe: 5 shares of XYZ stock, 10 shares of ABC stock). NOTE: the cash and stocks do not have to be repeated 18 times, as the beneficiaries share equally. Please simply list those entitled to distribution and indicate that they are each entitled to the specified amount of cash and the various stocks. 5) Please e-file a revise order with any changes to be made after addressing the issues above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BUENAVENTURA VINCENT ORTEGA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The judicial officer is to review and may discuss with counsel: 1) Should Bettr Law Group's dual representation of Felix Ortega and American Research Bureau (ARB) be allowed, in light of an insurmountable conflict of interest arising from Felix Ortega's agreement to assign 20% of his interest in this estate to ARB? See Estate of Wright (2001) 90 Cal.App.4th 228, 236 ["The interests of Gunning and Caudill are adverse, for example, concerning the reasonableness of the heir-hunting fee. The attorney representing the heir hunter cannot advise the estate beneficiary that the heir hunter's assignment is suspect or unreasonable"]. 2) If Felix Ortega or ARB intends to object to petitioner's accounting, new 15-day notice of their objection must be given to petitioner's surety, as the proof of service on file shows notice to the surety given on 06/03/2025, less than 15 days before this hearing. 3) Does a legally enforceable contract exist among the parties to equally share the estate? If no such contract exists, should Felix Ortega or ARB be allowed to move or petition to set aside a portion of the Court's July 2024 order (that admitted the will to probate) on extrinsic fraud or other equitable grounds not subject to time limitation and to contest the will for lack of testamentary intent? Issues requiring a supplemental brief from each party may include: i) Did the e-mail communications exchanged before the July 2024 hearing between the attorneys as authorized agents for respective contracting parties result in the formation of a contract to equally share the estate? If so, did the "Stipulation to Final Distribution of Estate", though not signed by Felix Ortega, merely memorialize the terms and conditions of an existing contract? ii) If the Stipulation was an offer from petitioner and Roberto Ortega III, did Felix Ortega accept their offer by refraining from objecting to the petition and contesting the will, even though Felix Ortega never signed and returned the Consent referred to in the Stipulation? See E.O.C. Ord, Inc. v. Kovakovich (1988) 200 Cal.App.3d 1194, 1201-1202. iii) Was Felix Ortega's signing of the Consent referred to in the Stipulation a specific mode of acceptance required by the offer? If so, was the offer revoked by the lapse of a reasonable time without communication of the acceptance? iv) Was Felix Ortega's signing of the Consent referred to in the Stipulation a condition precedent to petitioner and Roberto Ortega III's obligation to perform (to equally share the estate with Felix)? If so, was the condition waived or excused once the Court granted the petition and admitted the will to probate over no objection or contest from Felix Ortega? v) Would the circumstances leading up to the July 2024 hearing, as described in the parties' papers, support extrinsic fraud allowing the Court to partially set aside its July 2024 order to undo the admission of the will? See Probate Code sections 8007(b)(1) & 8226(a); see also Estate of Beard (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 753, 774-775. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JACK S LASNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing (All cash in the estate exceeding the FDIC insured amount was retained at one bank. In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. The Court will not sign orders where the last page includes only the signature line and there should be at least three lines between the last line of the order and the signature line. [LRSF 14.8 B.5.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY MARTHA CHAU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit will. No appearance required. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 07/13/2026.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES DALLAL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues have not been cleared: (1) The court will not set a minimum bond without adequate waiver of bond from the heirs. Either Waiver of Bond by Heir or Beneficiary [Form DE-111(A-3e)] on file or Petition should pray for full bond of $300,000. (2) Need the Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [JC Form DE-147S] to be conventionally filed. See the Local Rule of SF 14.18.E and 14.59.C. (3) Notice of Directions for Remote appearance and Access must be given. LRSF 14.1.C. (4) Each parent's written declaration to act and nomination of petitioner to act. They have higher priority. LRSF 14.24. (5) Proposed order must be submitted. LRSF 14.7. (6) Clarification is needed on whether attorneys Yip and Miller represent the petitioner together. The captions list both attorneys' names but include only Attorney Yip's firm, and the petition is signed solely by Attorney Yip, despite their firms being different. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

Please allege what property was left in San Francisco and specify the address in San Francisco.

Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing set for 7/14/2026. The personal representative shall file petition for final distribution.
